assets.name: lecture_02_figure_01.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 03 is the clearest fully composed title-card frame, but none of the candidates contain blackboard mathematics, a lecture diagram, or any board layout useful for note writing. This subtitle window should be rejected from the chapter figures.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: This program is brought to you by Stanford University.

assets.name: lecture_02_figure_02.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 01 is the clearest and least occluded version of this board moment. The full horizontal displacement vector, both endpoint marks, and the label for the field at x are all visible and sharp, making it the most useful documentary frame for reconstructing the geometry being discussed when Susskind says the two vectors are equal.

assets.name: lecture_02_figure_03.png

assets.name: lecture_02_figure_04.png
assets.subtitle_excerpt: What does the integral of d sigma mean?
assets.caption_hint: What does the integral of d sigma mean?

assets.name: lecture_02_figure_05.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 04 is the cleanest and most complete frame in this window. It shows the concentric-sphere layout with minimal lecturer occlusion, and the nearby mass-density expression is legible and more finished than in the earlier candidates. Candidate 03 is close, but the hand blocks part of the diagram; Candidates 01 and 02 are more obstructed by Susskind's body and shadow.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: The other side of the Gauss's equation is the integral of the component of A sticking out from the surface.
